domain: Senegalese gris-gris tied protective amulet (cloth bag with Quranic scripture + ritual objects; tied with cord to body; binding is part of protection; removal removes function)
specific_mechanism: Layered protective talisman whose protective function requires CONTINUOUS PHYSICAL BINDING to the wearer; removal breaks the protection.
llm_application: GRISGRIS-TETHER: a CRYPTOGRAPHICALLY-BOUND safety LoRA module — (i) safety LoRA is SIGNED with the base model's hash; (ii) every forward pass invokes a small verification gate that checks LoRA presence + signature match; (iii) if absent or modified, the gate emits NaN/refusal at the embedding layer, propagating downstream; (iv) the binding is cryptographic, not merely architectural — removing the LoRA cryptographically breaks output. Different from SaLoRA / SLIM / standard safety alignment because the binding is TAMPER-EVIDENT and removal causes IMMEDIATE OUTPUT FAILURE rather than silent safety degradation.
novelty_claim: SaLoRA, SLIM, identity-mixture LoRA preserve safety during fine-tuning but a stripped-LoRA model can still run with silent safety drop. Colluding LoRA attacks show silent safety bypass is possible. GRISGRIS-TETHER differs by CRYPTOGRAPHIC SIGNATURE + RUNTIME PRESENCE-CHECK + IMMEDIATE OUTPUT FAILURE on removal — the model REFUSES to operate without the bound safety LoRA. This is a tamper-evident hard-bound deployment regime.
